originalTitle: Cairo Takeaway secures court win over pro-Israel activist who claimed he was ‘completely vindicated’ after settlement
summary: Federal court judge rules Ofir Birenbaum breached parts of settlement with Middle Eastern restaurant reached after incident when working with Daily Telegraph
content: A pro-Israel activist breached parts of a legal settlement with Sydney restaurant Cairo Takeaway, a court has ruled, after he claimed to have been “completely vindicated” following the settlement. Justice Robert Bromwich handed down his decision in the federal court on Tuesday, in the latest chapter of a long-running feud with Ofir Birenbaum, who visited the popular Middle Eastern restaurant wearing a Star of David cap and necklace with reporters from the Daily Telegraph in February 2025. The operation, later revealed to have been dubbed “undercover Jew” internally by the newspaper, made international headlines after it backfired. In August, Birenbaum launched defamation proceedings against the restaurant’s owner, Hesham El Masry, and staff member Talaat Yehia for statements made in a series of social media posts. Birenbaum denied the version of events depicted in the posts, which the restaurant later deleted and apologised for before the defamation suit was launched. The case settled in March, but was reopened after the restaurant alleged Birenbaum had breached parts of the settlement the parties had agreed after he released a personal statement claiming he was “completely vindicated”. His lawyer, Rebekah Giles, also sent statements to the media in which she labelled the settlement “an important win for Ofir Birenbaum and the Australian Jewish community”, the court heard. In a May hearing over allegations that Birenbaum breached parts of the settlement, lawyers for Cairo Takeaway alleged the statements by Birenbaum and Giles were a “coordinated effort” to discredit the settlement and “control the narrative” in the media. “These were victory statements, they were planned, they were intended to bury the joint statement in the news cycle, and they had that effect,” Matthew Richardson told the court. Birenbaum’s lawyer, Kieran Smark SC, denied the comments were inconsistent with the official joint statement. He also suggested Birenbaum was vindicated because the restaurant had made a public apology. More to come
chineseTitle: 开罗外卖公司在法庭上赢得了亲以色列活动人士的胜利，后者声称自己在和解后“完全被证明是正确的”
chineseSummary: 联邦法院法官裁定奥菲尔·比伦鲍姆违反了与《每日电讯报》合作发生事件后与中东餐厅达成的部分和解协议
chineseContent: 法庭裁定，一名亲以色列活动人士违反了与悉尼餐厅 Cairo Takeaway 达成的部分法律和解协议，他声称在和解协议后“完全证明自己无罪”。周二，法官罗伯特·布罗姆维奇在联邦法院做出了裁决，这是与奥菲尔·比伦鲍姆之间长期不和的最新篇章。奥菲尔·比伦鲍姆于 2025 年 2 月与《每日电讯报》记者一起戴着大卫之星帽子和项链参观了这家颇受欢迎的中东餐厅。这一行动后来被揭露，被该报内部称为“卧底犹太人”，在事与愿违后成为国际头条新闻。 8 月，比伦鲍姆对餐厅老板 Hesham El Masry 和员工 Talaat Yehia 在一系列社交媒体帖子中发表的言论提起诽谤诉讼。比伦鲍姆否认了帖子中描述的事件版本，该餐厅后来删除了这些帖子，并在诽谤诉讼启动之前道歉。该案于三月份和解，但在餐厅指控比伦鲍姆违反了双方达成的和解协议的部分内容后，该案重新审理，而他发表了一份个人声明，声称自己“完全被证明无罪”。法庭获悉，他的律师丽贝卡·吉尔斯也向媒体发表了声明，称此次和解是“奥菲尔·比伦鲍姆和澳大利亚犹太社区的一次重要胜利”。在五月份关于比伦鲍姆违反部分和解协议的指控举行的听证会上，开罗外卖公司的律师声称比伦鲍姆和贾尔斯的声明是“协调一致的努力”，目的是抹黑和解协议并“控制媒体的叙述”。 “这些是胜利声明，它们是有计划的，它们的目的是将联合声明埋在新闻周期中，它们产生了这种效果，”马特
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
